acceptance.proof: One caller-selected provider is invoked once through a dependency-free trait and returns either typed provider failure or one version value with separately owned evidence; no ambient inspection or provider selection occurs.

The adapter accepts currentness only when returned provider identity, epoch, and revision exactly match an explicit caller-supplied owner-issued witness; it has no clock, expiry, arrival-time, or inferred freshness policy.

Accepted provider output validates the version through the existing implementation-assigned LSLC-001O bound and can move only that version into one LSLC-001S implementation-lane value without constructing or admitting a complete volatile snapshot.

Provider failure, invalid witness limits, identity mismatch, epoch mismatch, revision mismatch, and delegated version-bound failure remain typed, deterministic, and allocation-preserving where accepted.

The feature lock remains empty; runtime and transport lanes, host/environment inspection, clocks, sockets, devices, dependencies, platform effects, provider selection, complete S admission, and Manifold authority remain absent.

commit_policy: Commit the implementation-assigned version acquisition/evidence adapter separately from LSLC-001S complete admission, runtime/transport providers, host or environment inspection, clocks, networking, activation, devices, and authority.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the implementation-version provider, owner-issued witness, exact currentness check, S separation, and inert boundary.
Describe explicit caller-invoked acquisition and evidence without promoting it to runtime activation or general provider authority.
Review reusable evidence/currentness guidance; retain unchanged because exact owner-issued evidence and artifact-role separation are already required.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review installed skill without public mutation.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review repository-family routing; retain unchanged because LSL implementation ownership and Manifold exclusions remain intact.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review installed skill without public mutation.

non_scope: Any modification to accepted LSLC-001O/S source or evidence, Cargo manifests or locks, project or feature locks, oracle drivers, dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe Rust, native libraries, or external implementation-source inputs.
Runtime-assigned creation, identity, session, or host acquisition; transport-owned address or port acquisition; complete volatile snapshot construction or admission; XML or document representation.

System clock, wall time, monotonic time, expiry, duration, generated identity, environment variables, package metadata inspection, filesystem probing, host inspection, randomness, provider discovery or selection, retry loops, background work, process spawning, sockets, networking, protocol, wire, discovery, inlet, outlet, buffering, recovery, or FFI.
Runtime activation, Cargo feature, dynamic plugin, device, Quest, Hostess, platform lifecycle, package, permission, external integration, or product policy.
Manifold admission, registry, subscription, route, lease, revision, replay, identity, authorization, audit, command, session, topology, transport, provider epoch, or product-policy authority.
objective: Add one dependency-free, explicitly invoked adapter contract for acquiring only the implementation-assigned stream-info version from one caller-selected provider, retaining owner-issued provider identity/epoch/revision evidence and accepting currentness only by exact witness match, then permitting a separate move into the single LSLC-001S implementation lane without complete snapshot admission, runtime or transport acquisition, clocks, host inspection, networking, activation, devices, or Manifold authority.
outputs: A provider trait with one bounded synchronous acquisition call and an associated typed provider error; no registration, selection, retry, background, or ambient acquisition behavior.
Separate provider output and caller expectation types carrying bounded provider identity plus explicit owner-issued epoch and revision witness values.
An accepted acquisition wrapper that retains exact matching evidence separately from the opaque version allocation and exposes borrowed and consuming access.
A consuming projection that produces exactly one Version-labelled StreamInfoVolatileProviderValue for later caller composition into LSLC-001S, without invoking S or touching other ownership lanes.
Focused positive and damaged evidence for one-call behavior, exact witness matching, version bounds, allocation preservation, provider failure, and inert authority closure.
